column3: Title
The Trump administration on Tuesday took the first ​step toward loosening regulations ‌that protect the endangered North Atlantic right whale from ​vessel strikes.

Trump lashes out at Spain, says he is 'going to cut off all trade'. US President Donald Trump said Spain was "very uncooperative" and acted like a "terrible" ally after it declined to allow US forces to use Spanish bases for strikes on Iran.

Dept of Justice (DoJ) stated that it planned to renew its defense of executive orders that it had leveled against law firms, a sharp reversal a day after asking a court whether it could abandon the fight.
Law School Tells Students, ‘You MUST Be Aligned Politically With President Trump,’ For Summer Job and "GPA not a strong factor".

President Donald Trump criticised Prime Minister Keir Starmer for a second time this week, saying it was "sad to see" ​the so-called special relationship deteriorate after Britain initially held off providing support for U.S. strikes on Iran. Trump said he did not need Britain's support to strike ⁠Iran but said the delay had been disappointing. He told the Telegraph on Monday that Starmer had appeared to be "worried ​about the legality" of the strikes on Iran

Leaked Interior Department database reveals US plans to revise historical information. An internal government database revealed the scope of the Trump administration's effort to revise or remove information on African-American history, LGBT rights, climate change and other topics at hundreds of national park ‌sites.

Trump slammed Democrats for criticizing his administration’s strikes in Iran. He wrote on truth Social: “The Radical Left Democrats, a Party that has completely lost its way, are complaining bitterly about the very necessary and important attack, by the United States and Israel, on Iran,” and further “What most people understand is that they are only complaining BECAUSE I DID IT and, if I didn’t do it, they would be screaming — Why didn’t ‘TRUMP’ attack Iran, he should do it, IMMEDIATELY? There’s nothing surprising about this!”

More than two dozen contributors to the Reference Manual on Scientific Evidence for judges are raising alarm bells about political interference after the deletion of a chapter on climate science. The chapter was deleted after Republican state Attorney Generals in a letter requested its removal, claiming it was biased.

The US Department of Education has hung large banners outside its building in Washington DC, including one featuring an image of the late far-right commentator, Charlie Kirk. A banner next to the three figures reads: “Empowering our States to tell the Stories of our Heroes in American Education.” The banner featuring Kirk has sparked criticism as the commentator was a polarizing figure who had made incendiary and often racist and sexist comments during his career
Trump threatened Iran with “force never seen before” after the U.S and Israel’s strikes on the country,

Unaccompanied immigrant children who are pregnant, many by rape, are being moved to a single facility in Texas in order to avoid providing abortion services in a significant human rights violation, critics say.
President Donald Trump on Saturday celebrated U.S.-Israeli airstrikes that killed Iran's leader, but warned attacks would continue.

The Treasury Department has terminated its collective bargaining agreement with unionized workers employed at the Internal Revenue Service, the agency said Friday, in an escalation of President Donald Trump ’s push to exert more control over the federal workforce.
Trump urges regime change in Iran as part of his new attack on Iran
President Trump claims Iran interfered with 2020 and 2024 US elections, in part justification for launching an attack on Iran

The US and Israel have launched a war on Iran, with initial attacks on Teheran. Donald Trump declared the start of “major combat operations” and called on Iranians to rise up against their government. The US had been building up a large military presence in the region over the last few weeks. The war has no congressional approval and there is no UN security council approval

Health Secretary Kennedy announced two new committee members to join the CDC (Centers for disease control) Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices (ACIP). At least one of them is an anti-vaxxer and pushed back against COVID-19 vaaccination during the pandemic suggesting vitamins and steroids as treatments instead. ACIP had been schduled to meet at the end of February, but the meeting was rescheduled for March 18 and 19 instead.

The Trump administration has appointed a hard-right critic of Brazil's current government to a position shaping U.S. policy toward the South American nation, a move that suggests relations between the Western Hemisphere's two largest democracies remain delicate despite a recent rapprochement.

The Equal Employment Opportunity Commission’ said federal agencies can bar employees from using the bathroom that fits their gender identity. The EEOCs' role is to enforce federal civil rights laws against employment discrimination,primarily in the private secotor, but has some reach into the federal government.

Defense Department officials criticized Anthropic’s leader after the company on rejected their latest demand to allow the Pentagon unrestricted access to its most advanced model or face consequences. Officials from the State Department took to social media to reinforce the Pentagon’s case and chastise Anthropic, Senior Pentagon official Emil Michael wrote on Social Media “It’s a shame that @DarioAmodei is a liar and has a God-complex,”

Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth deemed artificial intelligence firm Anthropic a "supply chain risk to national security" and with immediate effect demanded that any contractors, suppliers or business partners of the Pentagon stop using its AI technology

Trump ordered all federal agencies to immediately stop using Anthropic's AI. In a Truth Social post he wrote: [the U.S.} “WILL NEVER ALLOW A RADICAL LEFT, WOKE COMPANY TO DICTATE HOW OUR GREAT MILITARY FIGHTS AND WINS WARS,” and said the “Leftwing nut jobs” at Anthropic “made a DISASTROUS MISTAKE trying to STRONG-ARM the Department of [Defense].” Anthropic had not given in to the Pentagon's demand for use of its technology to surveil U.S. citizens or empower autonomous weapons

Cuba’s communist government is facing a breaking point in its battle for survival under pressure from President Trump, whose energy quarantine against the country is aimed at collapsing the regime. Trump on Friday suggested the U.S. could achieve a “friendly takeover” of Cuba, perhaps mirroring America’s approach to Venezuela, where the military took out its leaders but kept the regime largely in place while demanding greater economic cooperation.

Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth ordered the cancellation of members of the military attending some of the country’s top-ranked colleges and universities on Friday, beginning academic year 2026-27, arguing the schools are teaching the “enemy’s wicked ideologies” to service members.

Netflix withdraws Warner Bros bid as Paramount's - led by major Trump donor and support - offer declared 'superior'. If Paramount Skydance is successful in its takeover attempt, it would own the news channel CNN as well as CBS News, sparking concern about concentrating news services within a small number of companies linked to Donald Trump's allies.

A veteran CBS News producer who is leaving the network after 46 years has suggested that political bias is at play at the network in a farewell memo sent to colleagues on Friday afternoon. She wrote "We’ve been told to aim our reporting at a particular part of the political spectrum. Honestly, I don’t know how to do that.”
Trump has claimed Iran is building missiles that could soon hit the US - building the case for attacking Iran. However sources say that those claims are not backed up by US intelligence.
US ambassador to Israel tells embassy staff if they want to leave they ‘should do so TODAY’, heightening fears of an attack on Iran

Scouting America will alter several policies at the urging of the Pentagon, including one targeting transgender youths, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth announced Friday as he pushes a campaign against military support for diversity, equity and inclusion efforts.

The Trump administration is slowly dismantling the federal disaster management system that protects the nation from chemical catastrophes, such as fires and explosions at high-risk facilities. The US Environmental Protection Agency’s (EPA) Response Management Program (RMP) requires more than 12,500 high-risk facilities to develop protocols to prevent catastrophes, or limit fallout, and was largely designed to protect workers, first responders, and fence-line communities. In 2024, Biden strengthened these protections. Under Trump, the EPA is now rolling back those rules and has targeted the Chemical Safety Board, which reviews accidents.

As more health systems end gender-affirming care for patients amid a crackdown from the Trump administration, scientists and advocates say the science of sex and gender is being misrepresented – and will have major repercussions for the healthcare for all Americans.
Donald Trump's administration asked the U.S. Supreme Court on Thursday to intervene in its effort to strip deportation protections from about 6,000 Syrians living in the United States.
The Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) fired 22 staffers who worked on "environmental justice"

Kansas rushed through a new law that demands that forms of identification must now reflect the credential holder’s “sex at birth”. The bill, known as SB 244, also bans transgender people from using bathrooms in public buildings that match their gender identity, and creates a sort of bounty hunter system, in which citizens can sue transgender people they encounter in restrooms for $1,000 in damages.
Activists who say they are in coordination with the White House are circulating a draft executive order that would unlock extraordinary presidential power over voting

A nearly blind refugee who spoke very little English, was found dead in New York days after immigration agents dropped him at a coffee shop alone. A border Patrol spokesperson said in a statement that "He showed no signs of distress, mobility issues or disabilities requiring special assistance,”

The Dept of Justice (DoJ) sued five states for voter data, three of them Republican-led (Utah, Oklahoma and West Virginia), bringing the total of states it has sued to 24, most Democrat-led. This is appears to be an escalating effort to seize the personal and private information of voters ahead of the midterm elections.
White House released an AI generated video of ice hockey player Brady Tkachuk insulting Canadians. The video appeared on TikTok with the notification that it was AI generated

US Department of Homeland Security (DHS) awarded a $250,000 public relations contract to a Republican political consulting firm led by former Trump campaign officials with connections to Corey Lewandowski, a senior adviser to the DHS secretary. This is a departure from federal procurement guidelines as the post demanded partisan loyalty.

Vice President Vance announced the Trump administration would “temporarily halt” more than a quarter-billion dollars in Medicaid reimbursements to the state of Minnesota. This is an escalation of Trump’s newly announced “war on fraud”. Vance said “What we’re doing is we are stopping the federal payments that will go to the state government until the state government takes its obligations seriously to stop the fraud that’s being perpetrated against the American taxpayer,”

Columbia University said on Thursday morning that one of its students was arrested by federal immigration officers who apparently misrepresented themselves to gain entry to a residential building to make the arrest. the student in question was understood to be Ellie Aghayeva, an undergraduate student who posted a photo that appeared to show her own legs, possibly in a vehicle, in an Instagram story with the message: “Dhs illegally arrested me. Please help”.

The U.S. Treasury Department on Wednesday imposed sanctions on more than 30 individuals, entities and "shadow fleet" vessels it said enabled Iran's illicit petroleum sales, ballistic missiles and weapons production.

Trump attacked Democrat Reps Ilhan Omar and Rashida Tlaib who had shouted "You are killing Americans" during his State of the Union speech. In a Social Media post he worte: "When you watch Low IQ Ilhan Omar and Rashida Tlaib, as they screamed uncontrollably last night at the very elegant State of the Union, such an important and beautiful event, they had the bulging, bloodshot eyes of crazy people, LUNATICS, mentally deranged and sick who, frankly, look like they should be institutionalized,”. He further said they should be sent back to where they came from.

FBI director Kash Patel fired at least 10 FBI employees linked to former Special Counsel Jack Smith's investigation into President Trump's retention of classified records after he left the White House in 2021.

US has been accused of “shameless exploitation” over a health financing agreement with Zambia worth more than $1bn (£740m). A leaked draft reveals that Zambia may accept terms worse than health financing agreements the US has reached with 16 other African countries. The terms include a commitment to give Washington access to its health data for 10 years andl also predicates any health financing on an even more covert arrangement that could open up the country’s mining industry to US interests.

President Donald Trump's administration has ordered U.S. diplomats to lobby against attempts to regulate U.S. tech companies' handling of foreigners' data. Experts say the move signals the Trump administration is reverting to a more confrontational approach as some foreign countries seek limits around how Silicon Valley firms process and store their citizens' personal information - initiatives often described as "data sovereignty" or "data localization."

President Trump’s nominee for surgeon general, Casey Means, on Wednesday said she won’t broadly encourage parents to vaccinate their children against measles, the flu or whooping cough, as previously done by her predecessors.

The Trump administration has hosted Tommy Robinson, the British hard-right extremist, for a meeting at the State Department in Washington. Robinson, whose real name is Stephen Yaxley-Lennon, said he was in America “making alliances and friendships”. The former football hooligan, who has convictions for assault, using a fake passport, mortgage fraud and contempt of court, was hosted by Joe Rittenhouse, a senior adviser at the State Department. He has described Muslims as “filthy scumbags” and said people should “make war” on them.
President Trump's State of the Union address was filled with propaganda and untruths.

The Pentagon has given AI firm Anthropic an ultimatum to abandon its ethics rules for AI. If Antrhopic doesn't comply, Pete Hegseth has threatened to terminate Anthropic’s contract with the Pentagon and designate it a supply chain risk. Anthropic refuses to allow its products to be used for mass domestic surveillance of U.S. citizens or in physical attacks where AI makes targeting decisions without human input

Defense Department said it was appealing an order barring punishment for Sen. Mark Kelly (D-Ariz) after a video in which Kelly and other Democrats pushed for service members to reject unlawful orders. U.S. District Judge Richard Leon had ruled to halt the Pentagon’s efforts to censure Kelly and lower his retirement rank
According to an NPR investigation, the Dept of Justice (DoJ) withheld some Epstein files related to allegations that President Trump sexually abused a minor.

Trump administration considers requiring banks to collect citizenship information. A possible executive order would be the latest development in the Trump administration’s broader immigration crackdown.
US sent fighter jets to Israel as part of the continued military build-up in the Middle East ahead of further negotiations with Iran

Department of Justice (DOJ) sued the University of California, Los Angeles (UCLA) accusing it of creating a hostile work environment for Jewish faculty, the latest instance of the Trump administration accusing colleges of failing to combat antisemitism during pro-Palestinian campus protests.
Dept of Justice (DOJ) sued the State of New Jersey and its Governor Mikie Sherrill over an executice order banning ICE from state property without a judicual warrant
U.S. military forces have boarded a third sanctioned oil tanker in the Indian Ocean, tracking it from the Caribbean Sea in a bid to target illicit oil connected to Venezuela.

President Donald Trump has lashed out at reports that his top military adviser had urged caution on air strikes against Iran, saying the general believes it would be "easily won". Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, Air Force General Dan Caine, has warned that strikes against Iran could be risky, potentially drawing the US into a prolonged conflict, US media report. In a lengthy post on Truth Social, Trump described the reports as "fake news".

The Trump administration is considering new national security tariffs on a half-dozen industries in the wake of a Supreme Court decision last week that invalidated many of the president’s second-term levies. These levies on industries could include large battery production, telecommunications, industrial chemicals and others and would be issued separately from a new global 15% levy.

Officials said that the Trump administration is stepping up work to uncover what it sees as malign foreign influence at U.S. colleges and universities, with the State Dept. assisting the Dept of Education (DoE) in the effort

A whistle-blower from the ICE training academy said that the ICE training program was “deficient, defective and broken” and had a pared-back curriculum. According to him, the cut-backs include a reduction in classes on the Constitution, legal system, firearms training, the use of force, lawful arrests, proper detention and the limits of officers’ authority. His testimony coincided with the release of  internal ICE records suggesting the Trump administration has curtailed the agency’s basic training.

The Education Department (DoE) announced plans to move two more programs to other federal agencies amid President Trump’s effort to “break up the federal education bureaucracy.”.HHS (Health and Human Services) will take some control of family engagement and school support programs, while the State Department will take on responsibilities for Section 117 foreign gift reporting requirements for universities.

Trump warned countries against backing away from recently negotiated trade deals with the U.S. after the Supreme Court struck down his emergency tariffs, saying that he would hit them with much higher duties under different trade laws.

Trump-appointed federal judge permanently barred the Justice Department (DoJ) from releasing a report by the special counsel Jack Smith detailing President Trump’s mishandling of reams of classified documents after he left the White House in 2021. Last month, Mr. Smith testified in front of Congress about several aspects of his election interference prosecution. But he has never been able to discuss his findings in the classified documents case publicly because Judge Cannon, who has repeatedly received lavish praise from the president, temporarily blocked the release of his report about it shortly before Mr. Trump returned to office.

In another attack on the security of the election, Trump during a meeting with families whose family members were killed by undocumented immigrants, that they would still be alive if the 2020 election hadn't been "rigged". This is the first time Trump has linked claims of a rigged election to the fates of people who had been murdered.
US says it struck vessel in Caribbean, killing three men

Donald Trump has declared that he can use tariffs in a “much more powerful and obnoxious way”, as the UK and the EU said they were seeking urgent clarity on the US trade deals they struck last summer.

Donald Trump renewed his condemnation of the U.S. Supreme Court on Monday after it ruled against his sweeping tariff program last week, vowing to turn to other tariff powers and licenses but giving no details. In his post Monday, Trump also pointed to the potential use of licenses to pressure countries. Trump used his social media post to again lash out against the justices who ruled against him, which included two who he had appointed during his first term in the White House.

President Donald Trump is curious as to why Iran has not yet "capitulated" and agreed to curb its nuclear programme, as Washington builds up its military capability in the Middle East, Trump's special envoy Steve Witkoff said. Trump has ordered a huge buildup of forces in the Middle East and preparations for a potential multi-week air attack on Iran. Witkoff said "They're probably a week away from having industrial-grade bomb-making material, and that's really dangerous." which could serve as pretext for a US attack in the coming weeks.

Denmark’s defense minister on Sunday rejected a plan by President Trump to send a “great hospital boat” to Greenland, the Arctic island and semiautonomous territory of Denmark that Mr. Trump has long sought to acquire. It was not clear why Mr. Trump planned to assist Greenland with its health care. Greenlanders have the right to health care that is free at the point of use, including prescription medications

A Guardian analysis of government records has found that the vast majority – 77% – of people who entered deportation proceedings for the first time in 2025 had no criminal conviction, exposing a stark gap between the Trump administration’s rhetoric and reality.

The Department of Justice (DOJ) fired the top prosecutor leading the U.S. attorney for the Eastern District of Virginia office hours after he took on the role to replace former Trump lawyer Lindsey Halligan. Attorney General Todd Blanche wrote on X, directed at Hundley, “you’re fired!” and “[Eastern District of Virginia] judges do not pick our US Attorney,” Blanche said. “POTUS does.”

Trump pulled his endorsment for Republican Jeff Hurd of Colorado, over a "lack of support" for his tariffs agenda, calling him a RINO (Republican in name only) and endorsing his rival Hope Scheppelman. Hurd was among a group of six House Republicans who joined with Democrats earlier this month to pass a resolution to repeal Trump’s tariffs on Canada

Trump called on Netflix to fire former United Nations Ambassador Susan Rice from its board of directors after she warned about an “accountability agenda” if Democrats regain power in the upcoming elections.He wrote in a Truth Social post "“Netflix should fire racist, Trump Deranged Susan Rice, IMMEDIATELY, or pay the consequences. She’s got no talent or skills – Purely a political hack! HER POWER IS GONE, AND WILL NEVER BE BACK. How much is she being paid, and for what???”

In recent months, the federal government has relentlessly prosecuted protesters, government critics, immigrants and others arrested during immigration operations, often accusing them of physically attacking officers or interfering with their duties. But many of those cases have recently been dismissed or ended in not guilty verdicts, often because official accounts of events are proven untrue. Criminal defense lawyers said it was unusual for federal prosecutors to pursue a high volume of charges over minor clashes with law enforcement, and that it was extraordinary to see the DoJ lose case after case across jurisdictions. However, those prosecuted will be harmed by the experience even if exonerated, which could deter others from protesting.

Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy announced that all truckers and pass drivers will have to take their commercial driver’s license test in English. While commercial drivers are required to demonstrate proficiency with the English language, many states allow them to take the test for their CDL in other languages.

President Donald Trump says he’ll increase his new global tariffs to 15%. The president is digging in on his trade war after yesterday’s Supreme Court ruling that duties he issued using sweeping emergency authorities were illegal.

Karen Newton, a UK citizen, was in America on the trip of a lifetime when she was shackled, transported and held for weeks on end by ICE (Immigration enforcement). She was travelling on holiday with a valid visa and has no criminal record.
The U.S. military said on Friday that it struck a vessel in the eastern Pacific, killing three men, the latest such incident in recent months.

President Donald Trump lashed out on Friday at the U.S. Supreme Court and the six justices who struck down his signature global tariffs - including two he appointed - in remarkably personal terms while hailing the three justices who backed him. Although previous presidents have sharply criticized Supreme Court rulings against them, Trump's lengthy tirade to reporters at the White House stood out for its contemptuous tone, as well as the personal nature of his scorn and praise.

New reporting says that Kennedy-aligned political appointees at the CDC fast-tracked a $1.6m grant for a randomized trial in Guinea-Bissau that would delay the hepatitis B birth dose for half of ~14,000 infants, bypassing the agency’s normal scientific/competitive review and raising internal alarm. The study drew international ethics criticism (including from WHO) and was paused/contested amid allegations the local ethics approval didn’t meet U.S. human-subjects requirements—while HHS/CDC and the investigators dispute wrongdoing and say review was appropriate.

Months before Renee Good’s killing at the hands of an immigration agent in Minneapolis set off nationwide protests, a federal officer shot and killed another American citizen in his car in South Texas, according to internal reports made public this week. The episode, which occurred around 12:40 a.m. on March 15, 2025, was reported by local media at the time as a shooting carried out by a law enforcement officer. It was not clear which agency was involved until last week

Harvard administrators pushed a faculty committee to cancel a long-running, biannual symposium featuring tenure-track women’s research, citing legal concerns about hosting a single-gender event. The cancellation comes as Harvard has scaled back diversity-focused programming amid heightened federal scrutiny. Last April, the federal government accused Harvard of disparate treatment in its hiring and promotion of white, Asian, straight, and male faculty. In the months that followed, Harvard renamed offices and revised administrative language to remove explicit references to race, gender, and equity.

The US’s ambassador to Israel, Mike Huckabee, has contended to the podcaster Tucker Carlson that Israel has a biblical right to take over the entire Middle East – or at least the lion’s share of it. “It would be fine if they took it all,” Huckabee said to Carlson during an interview posted on Friday. The Trump administration appointee and former Arkansas governor discussed with Carlson interpretations of Old Testament scripture within the US Christian nationalist movement.

The U.S. Department of Homeland Security (DHS) proposed a new rule which could pause work permits for asylum applicants until average processing times for certain asylum applications reach 180 days or lower. (Based on current wait times this could take between 14 to 173 years to reach the level to resume issuing work permits). Furthermore it seeks to restrict eligibility criteria for asylum-based work permits, arguing that a work permit "is not an entitlement" and is issued at the discretion of the DHS secretary.

Trump follows through on his threat and signs order imposing ‘temporary’ 10 percent global tariff after Supreme Court ruling. Trump is invoking Section 122 of the Trade Act of 1974, which allows the president to impose tariffs of up to 15 percent to address a “large and serious balance-of-payments deficit,” according to a White House fact sheet. Tariffs imposed under the authority may remain in effect for no more than 150 days unless Congress passes legislation extending them.

A detainee died in the custody of U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement in Indiana on Monday, with the cause of death under investigation, ICE said on Wednesday, marking at least the seventh death in 2026 in federal immigration custody. Lorth Sim, a 59-year-old Cambodian national, was being held at the Miami Correctional Facility. Staff found him unresponsive in his cell, ICE said in a statement.

President Donald Trump responded with fury to Friday's Supreme Court ruling that he did not have the power to unilaterally set tariffs on imports, denigrating individual justices as he vowed to wield a more restrictive law to continue his global trade war. He vowed he would not be deterred by what he repeatedly called a ridiculous ruling, announcing to reporters at the White House an immediate new tariff of 10% on imports from all countries on top of any existing tariffs, for up to the maximum 150 days allowed by Congress.

Following a flood of donations from hard-right donors in December, including Peter Thiel, Texas Congressional Candidate Jace Yarbrough was endorsed by Donald Trump on Truth Social. Yarbrough has recently articulated a series of extremist positions, including saying that  immigrants don't share the same values as Americans, called for a return to race-based immigration quotas, and that Islam is fundamentally incompatible with American citizenship.
The US Supreme Court has ruled that President Trump illegally used executive power to impose global tariffs

The Trump administration announced on Friday it will roll back air regulations for power plants limiting mercury and hazardous air toxics, a move it says will boost baseload energy but that public health groups say will harm public health for the most vulnerable groups in the US.

Cuba is confronting the United States’ first effective blockade since the Cuban Missile Crisis and running out of fuel fast, pushing the nation toward a humanitarian crisis and its government to the edge of collapse. The U.S. military has seized ships that have supported Cuba. And in recent days, vessels roaming the Caribbean Sea in search of fuel for Cuba have come up empty or been intercepted by the U.S. authorities. Trump's executive order last month threatening to impose tariffs on countries that provide oil to Cuba has also succeeded at scaring other nations from assisting Cuba.

The White House on Friday celebrated the Florida Legislature’s passage of a bill to rename Palm Beach International Airport after President Trump. White House communications director Steven Cheung praised the approval in a post on social platform X “Has a GREAT ring to it… President Donald J. Trump International Airport,” he wrote.
President Trump said Friday morning he is considering a limited strike against Iran if it does not forge a deal with the U.S. to constrain its nuclear program.

A US vaccine advisory committee meeting scheduled for later this month by the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) will not be held, with no new dates announced. Over the last year, Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. (RFK Jr) has stepped up efforts to rewrite national vaccination policy, including dropping broad recommendations for six childhood shots including COVID and hepatitis B, deepening federal support for states' vaccine exemptions, and cutting funding for mRNA-based vaccine research.

In an immigration ruling in West Virginia, the judge writes that "Across the interior of the United States, agents of the federal government—masked, anonymous, armed with military weapons, operating from unmarked vehicles, acting without warrants of any kind—are seizing persons for civil immigration violations and imprisoning them without any semblance of due process...It is an assault on the constitutional order"

World leaders who accepted the invitation of President Donald Trump to join his new peace board were greeted in Washington on Thursday with a mix of fanfare and personal flattery. Those who did not attend the inaugural meeting of the board in Washington received an ominous warning: "You can't play cute with me," Trump said. The president suggested that his new board could overtake the United Nations in prominence. Gaza is the board's first priority, but the Middle East conflict was not discussed in detail by Trump until 25 minutes into his welcome speech, as he focused first on complimenting the world leaders on the event stage, rating some of them on their good looks, wealth and strength.

During a speech at a steel facility in Rome, Georgia, Trump rehashed false allegations of voter fraud, and claimed that Republicans will never lose an election “for 50 years” if his allies in Congress pass the Safeguard American Voter Eligibility Act (SAVE) America Act, which critics have called the most restrictive anti-voting law in U.S. history.
Donald Trump warned Tehran that it had a “maximum” 15 days to reach a deal with the US or “bad things will happen”, as he weighs American military action against the Islamic republic.

The U.S. Commission of Fine Arts (CFA) on Thursday approved President Donald Trump’s proposal to build a ballroom on the former East Wing site, advancing a project that would create a structure nearly twice the size of the White House. The panel, whose commissioners were all appointed by the Republican president in January, voted 6-0 to approve the design. In an unexpected move, Chair Rodney Mims Cook Jr. then made a motion to grant final approval, rather than waiting until next month as originally scheduled.

President Donald Trump's administration has ordered the Federal Emergency Management Agency to suspend the deployment of hundreds of aid workers to disaster-affected areas around the country while the Department of Homeland Security is shut down. DHS, which FEMA is part of, entered a partial shutdown on Saturday, but has largely continued to operate since most of its functions are deemed essential. FEMA's mission is to help people before, during and after disasters, including hurricanes, tornadoes, earthquakes and floods. It brings in emergency personnel, supplies and equipment to stricken areas.

Controversial Christian nationalist pastor Doug Wilson preached at the Pentagon's monthly Christian worship meeting after an invite from Defense Secretary Hegseth. Wilson’s professed beliefs include that women should submit to their husbands, that the 19th Amendment (women's right to vote) should be repealed, homosexuality should be a crime and that Christians who owned slaves in the South “were on firm scriptural ground.”

The Trump administration announced on Thursday that it was reviving a proposal to prevent undocumented immigrants from receiving federal housing assistance, a policy that could displace tens of thousands of legal residents and citizens, many of them children, who live with unauthorized relatives in public housing. The Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) told public housing authorities across the country that they would face sanctions if they did not do more to verify the legal status of residents

A large banner featuring Donald Trump’s face was hung on the exterior of justice department headquarters on Thursday in a physical display of the president’s efforts to exert power over the law enforcement agency that once investigated him.

Jay Bhattacharya, the director of the National Institutes of Health (NIH), was named the acting director of the US Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC), making him the fourth leader in a year at the embattled agency in an unprecedented move that further consolidates power among a small group of men at the helm of US health agencies. Bhattacharya, who made a name opposing measures to prevent Covid-19, has previously strongly criticized officials who fund research and advise on policy simultaneously. Experts are concerned that Bhattacharya, who has been closely aligned with Kennedy’s agenda, will advance new restrictions on vaccination

The Trump administration has given immigration officers broader powers to detain legal refugees awaiting a green card to ensure they are "re-vetted," an apparent expansion of the president's wide-ranging crackdown on legal and illegal immigration, according to a government memo.

Donald Trump has nominated a lawyer who was part of his legal team in a landmark presidential immunity case before the U.S. Supreme Court, to serve as a judge on the St. Louis-based 8th U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals in Missouri. The lawyer, Justin Smith also represented Trump in his case against writer E. Jean Carroll, where a jury found he was liable for sexually abusing and defaming her.

The Equal Employment Opportunity Commission (EEOC) sued a bottler and distributor of Coca-Cola products it accuses of sex discrimination over an employee networking event that excluded men. The lawsuit against the Coca-Cola bottler is the first by the EEOC to claim that a diversity-focused workplace program is unlawful. The company hosted the event for about 250 female employees at a casino in Connecticut in September 2024, while President Biden was still in office.

After the Trump administration announced it would “draw down” federal immigration agents in Minnesota, attorneys and advocates say ICE is luring people into vulnerable moments - including using a “car trouble” ruse to get a mechanic outside before agents swarmed and arrested him - while shifting enforcement into suburbs and small towns. The accounts argue the public “drawdown” messaging is being used to lower people’s guard even as arrests continue with little DHS/ICE transparency, deepening fear and distrust in immigrant communities.

Trump again slammed the UK over its deal with Mauritius over the Chagos Islands. “Prime Minister Starmer should not lose control, for any reason, of Diego Garcia, by entering a tenuous, at best, 100 Year Lease. This land should not be taken away from the U.K. and, if it is allowed to be, it will be a blight on our Great Ally,” Trump said in a Social Media post.

The Federal Communications Commission (FCC) requested transcripts of Bad Bunny's Super Bowl halftime show performance from Comcast's NBC after a Republican lawmaker suggested it could violate federal indecency regulations, a member of the commission said Wednesday.

Immediately after a US border patrol agent shot two people in Oregon last month, the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) said the targets were “vicious” gang members connected to a prior shooting and alleged they had “attempted to run over” officers with their vehicle. In the weeks since, key parts of the federal government’s narrative have fallen apart. Immigration and criminal justice experts who reviewed the case records characterized the federal government’s communications as a “smear campaign” against the two Venezuelan immigrants, with mischaracterizations of their pasts and unsubstantiated allegations of criminality.

The Trump administration is eliminating four out of the six locations that had been slated for a practice test to try out new methods for the 2030 census, raising concerns that the U.S. Census Bureau might not learn enough about communities that have been traditionally difficult to count.

U.S. President Donald Trump said on Monday that Mexico would stop sending oil to Cuba as he ramped up a pressure campaign on the Caribbean nation. "Mexico is gonna cease sending them oil," Trump told reporters in the White House Oval Office. He did not elaborate on why he believed this to be the case. Reuters has reported that the Mexican government is reviewing whether to keep sending oil to Cuba, as it worries that Mexico could face reprisals from the United States over its policy to do so.

The Department of Energy announced it would exempt new experimental nuclear reactors from major environmental law requirements of the National Environmental Policy Act (NEPA). The act calls on federal agencies to consider the environment when undertaking new projects and programs, including the disclosure of how construction and operation might harm the environment, and it also typically required a written, public assessment of the possible consequences of a nuclear accident.

During an extended monologue about immigration on a podcast, Mr. Trump called for Republican officials to “take over” voting procedures in 15 states, though he did not name them.

The John F Kennedy Center, a world-class venue for the performing arts in Washington DC, will halt entertainment events for two years starting on 4 July during renovations, Donald Trump posted on Sunday on Truth Social. Masked federal immigration agents arrested a U.S. citizen driver at gunpoint in Minnesota, the epicenter of the Trump administration’s anti-immigration campaign, according to video footage from the driver’s dashboard. Conservative U.S. Supreme Court justices signaled they will uphold the legality of Trump's firing of a Federal Trade Commission (FTC) member, imperiling a 90-year-old legal precedent. A 1914 law passed by Congress permits a president to remove FTC commissioners only for cause - such as inefficiency, neglect of duty or malfeasance in office - but not for policy differences. If Trump wins, presidents could win unfettered power to terminate members of independent agencies at-will, which in turn could mark the end of their independence

Donald Trump proposed slashing fuel economy standards that Biden had finalized last year, in a push to make it easier for automakers to sell gasoline-powered cars. Trump repeats the right-wing claim that USAID subscriptions to Politico were payoffs. The $8 million payment in question was for subscriptions for Politico for the entire federal government, not payoffs from USAid.

Trump imposed sanctions on the International Criminal Court (ICC) after his visit with Israel's Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, leading to dozens of other nations pledging support for the ICC. In 2024, the ICC issued an arrest warrant for Netanyahu for alleged war crimes in Gaza.

EO (Executive Order) establishes a taskforce to combat "anti-Christian bias" in federal agencies and advise on policy implementations, renaming and changing the remit of the White House Faith Office to privilege evangelical Christianity over other religions

Attorney General Pam Bondi limits criminal enforcement of FARA (Foreign Agents Registration Act) and disbands the Foreign Influence Task Force, which had prosecuted Trump associates, including Paul Manafort and Mike Flynn, and investigated Russian interference in the 2016 presidential election

On January 31, 2025, Trump attempted to illegally fire the Federal Election Commission (FEC) Chair, Ellen Weintraub. Despite Weintraub and others pushing back against this illegal move, Weintraub was removed from the FEC.

White House directed the General Services Administration (GSA) to cancel all media contracts expensed by the GSA. More specifically, this action targeted Politico, BBC, E&E (Politico sub), and Bloomberg..

Executive Order (EO) "Keeping Men Out of Women's Sports"  bans use of federal funds for educational programs that allow transgender women and girls to participate in female sports; directs the Attorney General (AG) to prioritize actions against educational institutions that support transgender girls and women participation; pulls the U.S. out of international sports programs that allow transgender women’s participation; prohibits transgender women athletes from entering the U.S;. and instructs the Secretary of State to “use all appropriate and available measures” to ensure the International Olympic Committee adjusts its standards on participation in women’s sports. This EO discriminates against transgender women and girls.
Trump threatens to take control of Greenland, claiming it is needed for regional security. Denmark's prime minister says that Greenland is an autonomous territory of Denmark and not for sale.

Trump/U.S. Department of Justice (DOJ) fired prosecutors who worked on criminal cases against Trump, demonstrating Trump's unchecked power and starting an era in which the DOJ answers to the President.

Executive Order (EO) "Withdrawing The United States From And Ending Funding To Certain United Nations Organizations And Reviewing United States Support To All International Organizations" that the U.S. will not participate in the United Nations Human Rights Council (UNHRC), and will conduct a review of membership in UN Educational, Scientific, and Cultural Organization (UNESCO); and will withdraw funding for the UN Relief and Works Agency for Palestine Refugees in the Near East (UNRWA).

Under Elon Musk's leadership, DOGE (Department of Government Efficiency) accesses the Treasury Department’s central payments database, In violation of the privacy act and cybersecurity laws; the data breach threatens national security and exposes sensitive financial and personal information

Trump uses the power of the White House presidency to further his private businesses, which with any other government office holder or lawyer would be a violation of conflict of interest laws or professional code of conduct rules

Executive Order (EO) "Unleashing Prosperity Through Deregulation" requires that for each new federal regulation issued, at least 10 prior regulations be identified for elimination and agencies must offset the costs of new regulations with the elimination of prior regulations. Elimination of regulations has the potential for great harm, including, but not limited to, a wide swath of federally regulated areas such as online child safety and food and environmental safety.

Trump reiterated his threats to retake the Panama Canal as he claimed that China was "running the canal" violating the agreement when the canal was given back to Panama. This threat causes tensions between the U.S. and Panama.

Executive Order (EO) "Imposing Duties To Address The Situation At Our Southern Border" declares the opioid epidemic a national emergency and orders an additional 10% tariff on goods from Mexico and if Mexico retaliates there could be additional tariffs. This EO could start a tariff war with Mexico and it is dubious as to whether the President can legally use the National Emergencies Act in this instance. Feb 3, 2025 EO "Progress on the Situation at Our Southern Border" pauses tariffs on Mexico.

Executive Order (EO) "Imposing Duties To Address The Flow Of Illicit Drugs Across Our Northern Border" declares the opioid epidemic a national emergency and orders an additional 25% tariff on goods from Canada and if Canada retaliates there could be additional tariffs. This EO could start a tariff war with Canada and it is dubious as to whether the President can legally use the National Emergencies Act in this instance. Feb. 3, 2025 EO "Progress on the Situation at Our Northern Border" pauses tariffs on Canada.

Executive Order (EO) "Imposing Duties to Address the Synthetic Opioid Supply Chain in the People’s Republic of China" declares the opioid epidemic a national emergency and orders an additional 10% tariff on goods from China and if China retaliates there could be additional tariffs. This EO could start a tariff war with China and it is dubious as to whether the President can legally use the National Emergencies Act in this instance.
Criminal fraud investigation of Trump loyalist Andy Ogle withdrawn following Ogle's introduction of resolutions to allow Trump a third term and to support his takeover of Greenland

Trump sues CBC for Kamala Harris' October interview and despite CBS initially calling the lawsuit meritless, it now aims to settle the case. Editorial judgment is protected by the First Amendment and settling the lawsuit erodes First Amendment rights.

Federal Communications Commission (FCC) chair ordered an investigation the airing of commercials on NPR (National Public Radio) and PBS (Public Broadcasting Service) broadcast stations, which is prohibited. While the FCC does not regulate NPR or PBS, it does regulate the stations that broadcast NPR and PBS. The intended goal of the FCC chair is to have Congress defund NPR and PBS. Loss of NPR and PBS stations will especially hurt rural areas' access to local news.

Presidential Memo expanding the Migrant Operations Center at Naval Station Guantanamo Bay to full capacity (30,000) to provide space for high-priority criminal undocumented immigrants. Logistics, including costs and bed space, make this proposal concerning.

The Office of Personnel and Management (OPM) sent an email on January 29, 2025 to almost all federal employees offering them "deferred resignation." Staff must sign up by Feb. 6, 2025, will be paid through the end of the fiscal year (Sept. 30, 2025) and will not have to work past Feb. 6, 2025. Scant details beyond this were provided to staff, causing great confusion and anxiety for federal workers.

Executive Order (EO) "Additional Measures to Combat Anti-Semitism" calls report identifying all civil and criminal authorities or actions that might be used to curb or combat anti-Semitism, and inventorying all pending administrative complaints against or involving K-12 and higher education alleging civil-rights violations related to anti-Semitism. The result of this EO could be revocation of foreign students' and staff visas.

Executive Order (EO) "Ending Radical Indoctrination in K-12 Schools" requires that schools recognize only two sexes and requires they use the term "sex" and not "gender"; bans "discriminatory equity ideology"; requires "patriotic education"; requires a report on ending radical indoctrination. Among many other implications, this EO will limit classroom discussions on race, gender, identity, history, and social issues.

Executive Order (EO) "Protecting Children from Chemical and Surgical Mutilation" orders federal agencies to rescind or amend all policies relying on guidance from the World Professional Association for Transgender Health (WPATH); publish a review of literature on best practices for promoting health for children who have "identity-based confusion"; ensure that all institutions receiving federal funds end gender-affirming health care. This EO restricts access to gender-affirming health care and promotes criminalization of parents and health care providers involved in accessing this care.

Office of Management and Budget (OMB) memo temporarily freezes all activities related to obligation or disbursement of all Federal financial assistance, and other relevant agency activities that may be implicated by the Executive Orders (EOs), related to financial assistance for foreign aid, nongovernmental organizations, DEI, woke gender ideology, and the green new deal. Freezing these funds caused many employers with federal grants to layoff employees.

Executive Order (EO) "Defending Women From Gender Ideology Extremism And Restoring Biological Truth To The Federal Government" declares U.S. policy recognizes only two sexes; directs federal agencies to use “sex” not “gender,” requires that federal records and government-issued documents reflect sex assigned at birth; bans federal funds from promoting gender ideology and gender transition health care in prisons; and mandates single-sex spaces are designated by sex, not gender identity.

Executive Orders (EO) "Ending Radical and Wasteful Government DEI Programs and Preferencing" and "Ending Illegal Discrimination and Restoring Merit-Based Opportunity" end federal government diversity, equity, inclusion and accessibility (DEIA) programs, policies and mandates; revise union contracts to end DEIA practices; terminate all federal DEIA positions; instruct the attorney general (AG) and the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) director to submit a report on discouraging private sector DEIA programs; and instruct AG and education secretary to issue guidance to educational institutions to comply with the Supreme Court's affirmative action decision regarding college admissions. These EOs closed some federal offices, laid off many federal workers, and will negatively impact a wide swath of Americans.
President Donald Trump has said he believes the US will gain control of Greenland, after showing renewed interest in acquiring the autonomous Danish territory in recent weeks.

Trump threatens to take control of Gaza, relocate the 1.8 million Palestinians who live there, and redevelop the area into the "Riviera of the Middle East" under U.S. ownership. This would involve displacing 1.8 million people into countries that have not agreed to take them and potentially sending U.S. troops into the area, not to mention the enormous funds needed to turn the area into a "Riviera."

State Department issued a stop work order for all existing foreign aid government grants and contracts. The stop work order includes pausing all funding for the U.S. President's Emergency Plan for AIDS Relief (PEPFAR), which provides lifesaving antiretrovirals for 20 million people with HIV.

Despite a 2022 law requiring that Congress be given 30-days' notice for firing any Inspector General and that the White House can only fire an Inspector General for substantive reasons, Trump abruptly fired 18 Inspector Generals from over 12 different agencies. Inspector Generals are supposed to provide independent oversight for America and this move allows Trump to install those loyal to him in these vital watchdog roles.
Donald Trump insisted he was serious in his determination to take over Greenland in a fiery telephone call with Denmark’s prime minister, according to senior European officials.

Revocation of security details for former members of the last two presidential administrations (including Mike Pompeo, Anthony Fauci, John Bolton, and Brian Hook). Given that these former officials currently or previously had active threats, this move is very dangerous.

Memo signed by Acting Homeland Security Secretary Benjamin Huffman allows ICE agents to expel 1.4 million immigrants who had been allowed to remain in the US under two Biden-era programs for those seeking asylum under humanitarian parole programs.

Executive Orders (EO) Ending the Weaponization of the Federal Government and Restoring Freedom of Speech and Ending Federal Censorship order the Department of Justice (DOJ) to investigate the Biden administration. One calls for the DOJ to examine alleged misconduct during the Biden administration at the DOJ. The other calls for the Attorney General to investigate actions during the Biden administration to censor online free speech and ends the federal government's efforts to combat online dis- or mis-information.

Executive Order (EO) Enforcing the Hyde Amendment revoked two of Biden's EOs (14076 and 14079), which aimed to provide safety at clinics and pharmacies. Trump's EO limits access to abortion in the U.S. and globally by restricting funds for international organizations that offer access to or promote abortion.

Executive Order (EO) Council to Assess the Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) orders a review of FEMA. Co-chairs of the FEMA Council, Secretaries of Homeland Security and Defense, will advise President on FEMA capability, impartiality, and recommend changes to FEMA. Trump wrote this EO, declaring, "...we recommend that FEMA go away."

Executive Order "Clarifying the Military's Role in Protecting the Territorial Integrity of the United States" states that it is U.S. policy for the armed forces to protect the borders. The EO requires the Secretary of Defense to deliver a plan to seal the border in order to repel "forms of invasion including unlawful mass migration" within 10 days.

Executive Order "Strengthening American Leadership in Digital Financial Technology" concerns digital assets and blockchain technology and related technologies. Note that on this same day, the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) rescinded Staff Accounting Bulletin (SAB) No. 121 (SAB 121) removing  a barrier to financial institutions that offer digital assets. The Trump family and allies have invested heavily in crypto currencies

White House fired 3 Democratic members (Chairperson Sharon Bradford Franklin and members Ed Felten and Travis LeBlanc) of the Privacy and Civil Liberties Oversight Board (PCLOB), leaving the Board with one member, a Republican (Beth Williams). According to the American Civil Liberties Union (ACLU), this move "not only contradicts President trump's supposed commitment to addressing surveillance abuses, but is a direct attack on accountability and independent oversight."

Trump issued a presidential proclamation titled ‘Guaranteeing the States Protection Against Invasion.’ It declared that an ‘invasion’ is ongoing at the southern border and directed the Secretary of Homeland Security, coordinating with State and DOJ, to take ‘all appropriate action’ to ‘repel, repatriate, or remove’ individuals deemed to be engaged in that invasion

EO (Executive Order) issues a blanket pardon to and commutes the sentences of all January 6th, 2021 insurrectionists, pardoning all violent criminals and erasing criminal investigations into over 1,500 cases, the largest in U.S. history.
EO to remove covid vaccine requirements & provide covid safeguards
EO to discourage Diversity, Equity and Inclusion (EDI) initiatives in private sector & education
Bill introduced to raise taxes on university endowments from 1.4 to 21% on specific universities including Harvard, Yale and MIT, affecting universities' financial viability
EO (Executive Order) withdraws the U.S. from the 2016 Paris Agreement climate accord, a legally binding international treaty to combat climate change.

EO (Executive Order) withdraws the U.S. from the WHO (World Health Organization), stopping funding and assigning personnel. The WHO was established in 1948, following the 1945 formation of the UN (United Nations), to coordinate global responses to epidemics and improvements to mortality rates.

EO (Executive Order) renames the Gulf of Mexico the Gulf of America, and reverts the name of Denali to Mt. McKinley. In the case of Denali, the State of Alaska recognized that name in 1975 and requested the federal government do the same; it was recognized by Obama in 2015.

EO (Executive Order) promotes the use of capital punishment 'where possible.' It also directs the attorney general to keep inmates with sentences commuted by Biden 'imprisoned in conditions consistent with the monstrosity of their crimes' while checking whether they can be re-charged with state capital offenses, punishable by the death penalty.
EO (Executive Order) instructs the attorney general to not enforce a ban on TikTok. The ban was passed by Congress and signed into law in 2024, then upheld by the Supreme Court in 2025.

EO (Executive Order) 'restoring freedom of speech and ending federal censorship' bars the government from labelling speech as misinformation or disinformation, citing as censorship examples the Biden administration's attempts to refute lies regarding COVID-19 and the 2020 election.

EO (Executive Order) intensifies visa vetting of foreigners, including refugees, 'to the maximum degree possible' and requires agencies to compile a list of potential countries from which to suspend entry

EO (Executive Order) revokes security clearances of 51 ex-intelligence officials deemed Trump enemies, including Trump's previous national security advisor John Bolton, who had all signed a letter citing concerns regarding a potential Russian operation to influence the 2020 U.S. elections via email tampering on Hunter Biden's laptop.
EO (Executive Order) to limit birthright citizenship attempts to overturn the 14th Amendment, one of the most consequential amendments of the Constitution, ratified in 1868
EO (Executive Order) repeals array of Biden-issued orders, including aims to reunite migrant families, protect racial/gender equality and LGBTQ+ rights, block oil and gas leases, and many others

EO (Executive Order) 'Restoring Accountability To Policy-Influencing Positions' reinstates an EO from Trump's first term requiring that civil servants place loyalty to the president rather than to the Constitution
EO (Executive Order) unwinds the Inflation Reduction Act signed into law by Biden, crippling a major piece of climate legislation which reduced fossil-fuel use
90-day ''stop work' order for all foreign aid projects issued following EO (Executive Order) to freeze foreign assistance, ending life-or-death humanitarian aid operations

EO (Executive Order) expedites energy projects in Alaska, permitting the rollback of environmental protections in place since the establishment of the Arctic National Wildlife Refuge in the 1960s and passage of the Clean Air, Clean Water, and Endangered Species Acts in the 1970s
EO (Executive Order) to secure borders closes the country to migrants, including those seeking asylum and humanitarian parole
EO (Executive Order) establishes the U.S. DOGE (Department of Government Efficiency) Service by renaming and remaking the U.S. Digital Service

EO (Executive Order) ending civil rights or DEIA (Diversity, Equity, Inclusion, and Accessibility) programs includes overturning equal opportunity measures from the 1960s which mandate merit-based hiring
EO (Executive Order) ending asylum or USRAP (the U.S. Refugee Admissions Program) also blocks vetted refugees from entering the country

EO (Executive Order) ending the 'Weaponization of the Federal Government' authorises the Attorney General, Director of National Intelligence, and all other federal agency heads to review and investigate actions of the previous Biden administration

EO (Executive Order) recognizing only two immutable sexes bans recognition of gender identity and is to be enforced in spaces such as prisons and bathrooms, and with government-issued documents such as passports
EO (Executive Order) declaring a national energy emergency will bypass the Clean Water and Endangered Species Acts, in place from the 1970s, for military construction and energy production
Jeff Bezos' stance on Donald Trump evolves, with Amazon paying a $40 million licensing fee to produce a documentary about Melania Trump.
